reviews.text: I can't say enough good things about Brenda and her assistant Amy. I first met with Brenda when I was still in shock after my ex-husband left our family via text for his mistress. I interviewed several attorneys and immediately felt the most comfortable with Brenda. While I wasn't ready to file at that time, I was so grateful for her support and advice. A couple months later I did file for divorce and I couldn't have asked for better service. She is an excellent communicator and advocate. She answered questions/emails quickly and billing was transparent. She always made sure I was well prepared and understood every step of the process and exactly what to expect. I felt overwhelmed at some points, but always knew what I needed to do next. She helped me achieve the best possible outcome for myself and my kids. She also provided excellent advice when I needed the help of our local police department to stop my husband's girlfriend's harassment of me and my son. Truly she was part legal counsel and part counselor. I never thought I would find myself going through a divorce, but I couldn't have been in better hands. Don't hesitate to hire Brenda!

Mr. Holbrook is very responsive and always prepared . He answers all questions in a manner that someone without a legal degree will understand . He gives you the options and explains the consequences , and I appreciate that very much ! Thank you for the great service !
Awesome attorney. He is great attorney.
Awesome attorney. He is one the best in ogden.

He took the time to explain everything clearly and helped make a stressful situation much easier to handle. His fees were reasonable and fair, which I truly appreciated. His staff was also outstanding—friendly, helpful, and very knowledgeable. I highly recommend Zachary Holbrook and his team.

Zachary Holbrook runs a thorough and efficient firm.  Communication is top notch and you are never left guessing what’s going on.  If you need an awesome attorney to help you with your traffic or criminal case, he is your guy. His assistant Carina is also as good as they come. You will not be disappointed with them.

Once I met him, I knew he was the right person for the job. I reached out to Zachary C. Holbrook, P.C. for assistance with some legal issues, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the support I received. From start to finish, Mr. Holbrook and his outstanding assistant guided me through the complexities of the legal system. Throughout my case, Mr. Holbrook helped ease my anxiety by clearly explaining what to expect and keeping me informed about every possible outcome. Thanks to his diligent advocacy and genuine care, I achieved the result I was hoping for. He always set realistic expectations and fought tirelessly on my behalf. I highly recommend Mr. Holbrook to anyone facing a criminal case—his professionalism and dedication truly set him apart.

I contacted Zach with concerns about a situation my wife and I were experiencing.  Zach took the time to listen and to investigate the situation. He called us this morning and helped us to understand our options.

reviews.text:
Matt Schmoldt...what started out as a “shot in the dark” call became an incredible journey through a difficult and painful accident, and following recovery.

Matt became a good friend, someone who genuinely cared more about what was best for me than the accident and how to "win".

Matt listened and supported me when I was unsure and needed time to see if I was ready to move forward, if my family was ready.

Matt was gentle and supportive, he truly heard my concerns and fears and always advised me to do what was right for me and my family.   As gentle as he was with me, he was equally as fierce, educated, articulate, and unwavering in his demands against the other party.

Matt provided the feeling of safety, of being able to just put it all in his hands and know he was there for me and he would take care of everything.  He was like a protective big brother who had a genuine interest in me and making sure I was okay.

I would highly recommend Matt Schmoldt and Creekside Legal for any needs you may have.  I will use Matt any time I find a need and refer him without hesitation to friends and family.   Great experience from beginning to end!!    Thank you Matt!!!

reviews.text:
Experiencing a car accident is undoubtedly one of the most stressful events one can go through. The chaos, confusion, and emotional toll can feel overwhelming, leaving you uncertain about what to do next. Thankfully, my experience was made significantly easier thanks to my lawyer, Matt.

From the moment I reached out to him, Matt provided reassurance and clarity during a tumultuous time. He guided me through the complex legal processes, ensuring that I understood each step and alleviating my worries. The other driver denied responsibility, despite overwhelming evidence, including videos from multiple sources and an undercover police officer as a witness. I often thought about how much worse the situation could have been without his expertise and support.

While the aftermath of the accident was no picnic, Matt’s professionalism and compassion made a daunting situation manageable. He went the extra mile to ensure that the car insurance companies understood that I was not at fault, even when I switched my insurance carrier. He took care of the heavy lifting, allowing me to focus on my recovery. I’m truly grateful for his help; he transformed a stressful ordeal into a process that, while still challenging, felt navigable and supported. Highly recommend!
reviews.ownerResponse: Nikki, you have been a fabulous client to work for.  Thank you for your kindness, patience, and great attitude.  It has been a true pleasure and I hope year 2025 treats you so kindly!

I had an outstanding experience with Craig Swapp & Associates. From day one, they stepped in and handled everything so I didn’t have to battle with insurance companies or chase paperwork. That let me focus on what mattered, getting better.

Communication was excellent. Every question I asked was answered quickly and clearly, and I always knew where my case stood and what to expect next. I never felt in the dark or like I was just another file on a shelf.

I especially want to recognize Tabitha Santos. Tabitha was the steady voice throughout my case, proactive, responsive, and incredibly thorough. She checked in regularly, explained each step in plain language, and made sure I felt supported the whole way through. Her professionalism and genuine care turned a stressful situation into a manageable one.

If you’re looking for a team that has your back, keeps you informed, and takes the weight off your shoulders, I highly recommend Craig Swapp & Associates. And if you get the chance, ask for Tabitha, she’s exceptional.

reviews.text:
I was in a car accident in 2023 directly in from of my office. The car in front of my was driving the speed limit when they suddenly braked and stopped. Unfortunately the car behind me didn't see me at a full stop and hit me from behind, totaling my Lexus SUV and causing injuries I'm still recovering from. I was the "sandwich" car. Fast forward to me contacting The Advocates for assistance.

I'm leaving an honest review, so I'm leaving MY experience with the firm. Initially, I had a difficult time getting anyone to answer my calls. But when I did get my call answered the atty I initially connected with was incredibly kind, concerned, and polite.

We started the journey, but due to his case load. I was redirected to my current atty. This was the absolutely the BEST thing that happened to me. I'm so proud to say that Atty Bradley Johnson took over my case and has been my saving grace in more ways than I can count. I've worked with other attys for different situations, and have honestly felt "dirty" after dealing with them.  Not the case with Mr. Johnson. This man embodies everything you'd want in an atty and in your life. He empathetic. He's HONEST.  He's kind. He works hard for you. He keeps his word. In other words, he's ETHICAL! I'm so grateful for all he's done for me. And he has gone above and beyond to help me.

I highly recommend if you need an atty to help you after a car accident you contact The Advocates and ask for Mr. Bradley Johnson to represent you. You won't be disappointed. Thank you Brad for all you've done for me!

Lori

reviews.text:
About 10 months ago, I was involved in a near fatal motorcycle accident. Right out of trauma one and sitting in the ICU I hit up Google looking for an attorney to represent me against the at fault party who’s negligence almost took my life. But I kept getting call centers, and the experience started feeling more like trying to get a hold of Walmart Customer service than  seeking the services of a professional office. Then Steve Linton at The Advocates answered the phone, In person, at 5 AM in the morning!!

Now, just about 10 months later, I have been fully indemnified and then some. My body will never be completely whole again, but thanks to Steve Linton and the amazing back office staff at The Advocates who support him, my wife and I have our life back. A life that the negligence of the at fault party so carelessly took away from us for almost a year. Thank you, Steve, Julie, Angie, and the rest of you there at The Advocates who took care of my wife and I with professional care and attention until this matter was finally Resolved.

The included photos? What a life made whole again really looks like after the trauma and the paperwork are done. Thank you, Steve. The settlement you recovered for me was substantial. The life you returned to me. Priceless.
reviews.ownerResponse: Willcathe, we are honored to hear how Steve's team helped restore your life after such a traumatic accident. Your recovery and happiness mean everything to us. Thank you for putting your trust in us and for your kind words.

Seeing as I called for a simple question, I deemed it not necessary to give my actual name(usually dont unless necessary). This question should have had a simple yes or no answer if you have experience in divorce like they say. I filed for divorce, and they give you a packet of rules(the one in question being not turning off your spouse's phone/number) at the courthouse when you file. My question was, if I give my spouse a 30-day notice to move to their own account before doing so, is that legal? The guy was rude and rushed me to get to the question when I was trying to explain important details pertaining to it. Then, he stated he would need to know more about the case to answer. Well, which is it? Then, he asked if I had someone representing me. Seems to me he just wants to get you in and get your money. I just told him I'd call the court when they opened the next day and ask them as they'll be able to give me an answer. This is why I filed by myself, though. Lawyers(especially ones like him) make me feel like it's a waste of a lot of money for something I can do/figure out myself. Even if it was a contested one and i needed a lawyer, I certainly wouldn't be going to him after this experience.

I had a pretty frustrating experience with this law firm. From the start, communication was really lacking. I found myself waiting a long time for responses to simple questions, which was surprising given how new I was to navigating legal matters. I tried to be understanding, thinking maybe they were just busy, but it was hard to ignore how often I'd reach out without getting answers.

When I finally managed to ask to speak to my lawyer, John Cummings, after nearly four years, he couldn't even spare me a quick chat. My first case was unfortunately dropped due to the statute of limitations. I understood that slip and fall cases can be tough, so I didn't push too hard. However, my second case seemed like a clear-cut situation—the other party had been ticketed for an auto/pedestrian accident, and their insurance was willing to settle.

I was reassured that things were moving forward, but weeks and months went by with no updates. When I finally did hear back, they seemed to place some of the blame on me for delays. Overall, I felt let down and frustrated with how the entire process was handled. I really hoped for better support and communication throughout my cases
I finally decided to switch to a new law office after feeling stuck for years. Honestly, I was at my wit's end worrying about whether my case would be settled before the statute of limitations ran out. I had waited for one last update from my previous attorney, but they kept pushing back the timeline and never followed through. It was so frustrating.

Now, the new law office has been reaching out for weeks to get information from them, and I feel like I'm back in the same boat with no responses. I just hope others don’t have to go through the same stressful situation I faced during one of the toughest times in my life. If only I had taken the time to read reviews before making my choice. Does  anyone has had similar case with me? Are these people real layers? If so, do you think they can represent you and serve you as you wish? Noooo. Learn from me. If you go to these guys for any legal advice, you will lose your money, time and more importantly lose your mind. Avoid this firm.

Right before Christmas 2023, our family was t-boned by an individual running a stop sign. The events that followed were a whirlwind! Hospital visits, calling insurance agencies, and attempting to handle the emotional fallout from the accident was more than we could handle, especially over the Christmas holiday. We had no clue how to handle such a situation.

Luckily, we were referred to Logan Magnusson from Nance Law. I cannot recommend him highly enough! From the very first consultation, he made our family feel heard and supported, taking the time to thoroughly explain the legal process and answer all our questions. His professionalism, dedication, and expertise in personal injury law was clear every step of the way.

Logan not only handled our case with exceptional skill, but he also showed a genuine care for our family’s well-being, making the entire experience far less stressful than I anticipated. He kept us updated on every development, and his attention to detail was outstanding. Thanks to his hard work, I was able to get the compensation we deserved.

If you're looking for an attorney who is both knowledgeable and compassionate, Logan Magnusson is the one to trust. I am truly grateful for everything he has done for me and my family! We will forever be calling on him if and when these types of situations arise. We are not only clients but now lifelong friends. Thank you, Logan, for everything!

He spent nearly 2 hours going over options, researching the law, even contacting agencies that make expungement recommendations before finally learning that the arson charge simply wasn’t eligible for an expungement. He easily could’ve charged me for the process of reducing both convictions prior to the expungement process, but he was upfront and honest in telling me that it would be a waste of time and didn’t charge me a penny. We recently had the opportunity to work with Vincent, and I would advise anyone who is seeking his services to absolutely not throw your money away, very well might be the weakest lawyer I have ever seen or talked to, I found out pretty quickly he has no idea how to argue outside the box or make a common sense argument, various things you would think would be in our favor from the cop who never actually found what he was looking for never even stepped foot in our house, to the child protective services opening and closing a case saying no problem found, to the adult probation board who the courts put in place to recommend punishment, recommended nothing more than a ankle monitor, and we still got jail time, and a crazy amount of probation, Oh and by the way the defendant had never even had as much as a speeding ticket, 1st time offender in every sense of the phrase, we pretty much just got to sit back and watch him get smoked by the prosecuting attorney, if you are wondering what the inside of a jail cell looks like by all means hire these guys. reviews.ownerResponse:
Jules-
I would like to address your review with the preface that I have never represented you personally. While I may have represented your daughter all information is protected under attorney client privilege. I know the legal process can be frustrating, especially for a third party that does not have all of the information. In all instances, Stevens and Gailey is focused on understanding all circumstances surrounding their clients. We cannot control extrinsic factors which will make a current case difficult to manage. This occassionally happens where a client will come to our office seeking assistance and have a prior history of criminal activity, continue to make choices which violate the terms of the plea agreements, and often commit additional criminal violations which cause future cases to be filed or involve unrelated charges for a separate incident. These continued activities by clients can result in very difficult and often statutory consequences during sentencing. We clearly outline options for our clients in each case based on the specific details of their case and require a retainer to handle the case. This would also be true with your daughter as it is a standard practice. If she had done any of the above mentioned behaviors or was delinquent in payment I would have to review her case with her as she was the one whom I represented. Your daughter is welcome to call me personally and discuss her case with me. Each of our clients is aware that we spend considerable time consulting with them individually and representing them fully in several court hearings. Our clients understand their options and are in control of how we proceed with their case. Please consider removing your review and having your daughter reach out to me personally to discuss this matter. We look forward to hearing from her.

reviews.ownerResponse: I am sorry you feel that way, we met went over the evidence and watched the video, we went over the mandatory sentencing guidelines, I told you what you would be sentenced to and I agreed to ask for your deviations, none of which the Judge granted.  I was not late to the hearings to my recollection but I am frequently in with prosecutors speaking about cases or in other court rooms and they will not call the case with out me there.  Your footage is still available on the the secured website.  As for the prosecutors being friends, we are more acquaintances, I know what they will and wont do from 27 years of working with them.  I have not received any requests for additional information since your were sentenced.  I wish you the best and if you want to bring me a flash drive I will happily make you a copy of your entire file for your records including the video.

reviews.text: Hiring Roy Cole for my divorce was the worst mistake I made. My problems would have been avoided if I simply would have hired a below average lawyer or even represented myself. Because of his negligence and poor work ethic, he missed a critical typo before having me sign the divorce papers which granted my abusive ex husband more custody then agreed to in mediation. I think he does a poor job so cases get prolonged and he can charge more money. He kept charging me more money to fix HIS ERROR. Only a dishonest lawyer would keep charging a client for an ERROR HE MADE! Any other profession would fix the mistake at no cost or provide a refund. He did neither. So If you want to waste money, miss critical deadlines, prolong your divorce and have an attorney that can be walked all over by the opposing side, hire Roy Cole. He also wouldnt fight for me with the sale of my house. I wanted 50%, he had me settle on a dollar amount. Well my ex got almost $100,000 and i got $34,000.
reviews.ownerResponse: I am sorry you feel that way, we both know the truth, that they changed the language after we reviewed it, and I filed every document I could to get it set aside.  Your problems would have simply been avoided if your ex and his attorney had been honest and not changed wording after we approved it.  You chose to go to a different attorney who charged you even more money to fix the issue I was already in the process of fixing.  You then filed a false claim with the Bar against me claiming you had never received a single invoice, I am glad I kept my records to show you were lying, your complaint was dismissed.  I hope you got the matter resolved with your ex and I wish you nothing but the best and a happy life.  Also you seem to have forgotten I quit charging you because of your sad story after they forged the document, maybe you should review his attorney.

What truly sets Justin apart is his compassion and dedication to clients. He took the time to listen and empathize with my concerns, creating a supportive environment that made a significant difference during a stressful time.
I wholeheartedly recommend Justin. He is a true advocate for his clients and will go above and beyond to achieve the best possible results.

Justin brings a calm and concise approach to a highly contentious situation, and he does this with such compassion and respect that you feel a measure of relief in one of the hardest moments in life. I’m so very appreciative of his knowledge and skill in navigating this complicated legal process and would highly recommend Justin. I wish I would have started working with him from the beginning!

reviews.text:
Negative 2 star review

I regret hiring this law firm and working with Jaycee Webb. As a 56-year-old man going through a divorce, I needed competent legal support, but what I received was far from satisfactory.

Initially, I retained this firm for $5,000, with the understanding that my primary concern was to address a protective order against me before any work on the divorce proceedings. Unfortunately, Jaycee failed to issue a counter-protective order. This negligence allowed my wife to pile on numerous orders against me, compounding my troubles instead of alleviating them.

The representation was minimal at best. Jaycee attended only one mediation session for me and provided no other legal advocacy. Her excuses for not receiving responses from the opposing counsel were frustrating and unacceptable. Eventually, I felt compelled to halt all work until I found new representation, which I should have done much sooner.

To make matters worse, it was evident that Jaycee was unprepared during the mediation, demonstrating a complete lack of professionalism. My new attorneys from Kauffman and Kauffman managed to correct her mishandlings in just one month, achieving results that Jaycee couldn't. They described my situation as having "handed them a dead carcass," which highlights just how poorly my case was handled by her.

Jaycee also had the audacity to charge me $4,700 for no emails she sent to me with excuses that she couldn’t get anything done.  She has also put a lien on my house for $4700. I should be able to take her to small claims court or have some kind of legal recourse. If anyone has any advice for me, I would welcome it.

My experience with Jaycee has been nothing short of a nightmare, and I will ensure my complaints are shared widely across all possible platforms to warn others. Save yourself the frustration and find a more competent attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Donnie, we are sorry to hear that you are dissatisfied with your experience. Due to our duty of confidentiality, we cannot discuss the specifics of your matter in a public forum. Legal options, potential risks, and the realities of the legal process were clearly explained at the outset of representation and as your case progressed.

Legal matters, particularly those involving family law, can be complex and emotionally challenging, and outcomes are influenced by many factors outside of any attorney's control.

Retainers are deposits applied to services rendered, as outlined in our written retainer agreement. This was clearly discussed at the beginning of representation and in response to your questions.

We remain committed to providing professional, diligent representation to our clients and stand by the work performed in this matter.

reviews.text:
Truly a bizarre experience. We paid our $3000 retainer to file a Motion to Enforce with the courts in a child support case. We received an invoice for over $1200 before the motion had even been filed. Then our lawyer up and left the firm without any warning whatsoever a week after taking my case. Apparently he just decided Family Law wasn't for him... you can read into that however you want. Either they employed someone unstable, unreliable, and uncommitted to a very sensitive line of work, or he saw something he morally didn't agree with and felt he had to leave in a hurry... I don't know. Either way it was a concerning reflection of the firm in general.  Unbeknownst to me they assigned me to a different lawyer. There was a communication breakdown relaying this information to me and I ended up finding out my original lawyer was no longer at the firm when I couldn't find him on their website anymore. Did I mention that this was three weeks after my original lawyer said in our consultation that my Motion to Enforce needed to be filed right away? Still hadn't happened...

They offered me a third lawyer after the first two lawyer's actions left me scratching my head. However, I told them I'd pay the $275 for the hour long consultation with the initial lawyer, but that my confidence in their abilities to represent me in something as critical as family law was shaken. I felt like the remainder of my retainer should be refunded since the Motion to Enforce was never actually filed as promised. They told me they'd extend a $500 "discount" because of the circumstances, but that they would not refund the remainder of the $1200 I'd already paid. They said they actually should have charged me more because the lawyer who contributed in the communication break-down eventually talked to me about my original lawyer leaving without notice... They actually wanted to charge me to relay information about their own lawyer's unprofessional conduct. Truly, truly mind-blowing.

So, long story short I paid roughly $700 and lost almost a month's worth of time and energy on a time-critical filing because of this firm. It caused a lot of stress and frustration only to find myself back at square one with absolutely nothing accomplished or to show for it. Do yourself a favor and find another firm to work with.

UPDATE IN RESPONSE TO FIRM’S RESPONSE: Regardless of what the firm said in their defense, when the second lawyer couldn’t reach me by phone he could have attempted to email me, or try my phone a second time to relay that my original lawyer had left the firm. That’s a pretty big issue that would require confirmed communication. Their response also said that the second lawyer did work in the interim responding to the opposing counsel, justifying additional charges to my account. This is false. There was no opposing counsel at that time. My ex did not secure representation until a month after I discontinued doing business with this firm. Additionally, the Motion to Enforce, which was the whole reason I employed them, was still never filed at the time I discontinued doing business with them. If the work they supposedly did was necessary to file the motion, fine. But they didn’t do that. They just sat on a time-sensitive filing. I lost my faith in lawyers after this experience and ended up researching how to file the Motion to Enforce myself. I did so and arrived at the outcome I had initially desired. Mind you, I have absolutely no background in law and my ex did secure legal representation at that point. It was me vs. a lawyer and I still had a desired outcome. So, it’s not like this was some really complicated case that required tons of legal prowess. It seems to me that this firm would like to make people think that they are doing more than they are so they can bill more. At the end of the day, they failed to fulfill the objective they were hired for and were lacking in communication. So, I stand by my original review. I would recommend finding a different firm to work with.
reviews.ownerResponse:
UPDATE: This matter dates back to June 2022, and your account is not accurate. Opposing counsel entered the case in May 2022, and our firm was retained the following month. The communication breakdown occurred because our attorney reached out and left you a voicemail message, which unfortunately was not returned for two weeks. In the meantime, our attorney filed an appearance to ensure nothing was missed and worked with the court to correct an error regarding opposing counsel's representation. You were never billed for that work; the only charges were for time spent by your initial attorney, which we reduced with a significant courtesy discount. Despite our efforts to resolve this fairly, you continue to post negative reviews over three years later. We stand by the integrity of our firm and the accommodations we extended, and we wish you the best moving forward.
--------
We understand that the sequence of events you described was highly unusual and not in line with our standard of exemplary service. I want to apologize for the challenges you faced during this process. The sudden departure of your original attorney was an unfortunate situation that took us by surprise, and we deeply regret any confusion and inconvenience this may have caused.

The attorney from our firm who took over your case did, in fact, reach out and left you a voicemail message. I don't have an explanation as to why your receipt of his message was delayed, but it may have been due to your travels. Although we had no control over your receipt of our attorney's message, we did our best to address the situation as soon as we were made aware of it.

Regarding the billing concerns, we did extend a discount as a gesture of goodwill, recognizing the unique challenges you experienced. I suspect that there was an unresolved misunderstanding regarding the charges, as my email stated that our attorney who was assigned to your case did additional work on your case that you were not billed for. The additional work was unrelated to your original lawyer's departure and was due to something that opposing counsel filed with the court. Our attorney took care of this while he was waiting for you to return his call, and again, you were not billed for his work. I personally did everything in my power to help you, and I am sorry that we didn't meet your expectations.
We believe this review was meant for a different office, but we are happy to address any concerns you may have if you'd like to respond or send us an email. Thank you.

reviews.ownerResponse: The individual who posted this review was not a client, and the statements in this review, including the hourly rate and other claims, are entirely false and fabricated. If you need assistance with a divorce or custody matter, consulting with one of our knowledgeable family law attorneys will help you prepare for the road ahead.

reviews.text:
UNEXPERIENCED and WASTE OF MONEY This place is the absolute worst experience of my life!!! Did a phone consultation and seemed like a good place to help out with my first ever divorce. I was very WRONG!!!
Mohammed made it seem that he was going to take care of me and really cared about my situation (divorce).  From the get go I should have seen the red flags (many of them) his last attempt for me to refill my retainer is when I finally noticed to be done!!! His soul goal was to make sure his bank account was satisfied and nothing else mattered. Please do your research on divorce lawyers (especially if it’s your first ever divorce) I was definitely taken advantage of and was charged over $6k for absolutely NOTHING!!! Countless time on meetings and phone calls that lead nowhere and he claimed he wasn’t getting response from other lawyer involved and made many excuses why it was taking so long. Never once mentioned mediation in the beginning.
MEDIATION should 💯 percent be done first thing before any lawyer involved
I ended up firing Mohammed and went to mediation and offered exactly what we were planning to offer and was done in 4 hours and only had to spend $550. Now I am in a huge $6k hole (nothing received for this money) and I should of done more research instead of doing what most people think is right(hire a lawyer who should have experience and knowledge)
PLEASE do not waste your money or time with this firm. I feel like I definitely got conned out of $6k.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We value honest feedback and want to clarify the situation. You requested that your past-due balance be written off in exchange for a positive review and later threatened a negative review when we did not comply. We made it clear that we do not respond to threats and that our priority was ensuring the accuracy of both the work and billing.

Your attorney performed significant work to prepare your case for mediation and made efforts to settle it beforehand at your request. You repeatedly expressed gratitude for his assistance, including an unbilled phone call on a Sunday evening.

The only specific billing concern you raised involved time spent drafting a stipulation, which was valid and billable despite opposing counsel deviating from the agreed plan. In good faith, we offered a courtesy credit for that time as a potential resolution to your concerns, which makes your review especially disappointing.

Our firm completed valid work and upheld integrity throughout. We remain committed to providing quality representation and transparent communication.

reviews.text: Incredibly professional and helpful group of individuals. Mr. Rand Lunceford has always been very kind and understanding. I appreciate the time and effort this office has put into helping me. Highly recommend reaching out to them if you need assistance.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your review. We're so glad to know that Rand and our team were able to provide the professionalism and support you needed. Your appreciate means a lot to us, and we're grateful for the opportunity to help.

reviews.text: I've been meaning to express my gratitude for a considerable time.  Last year, Andrew took on my case concerning some deeply upsetting events, providing unwavering support and readily responding to my emails and calls.  It was an incredibly difficult period; my custody agreement was being modified, and my children were removed from my care, but Andrew secured their return.  The journey was arduous, but he helped me rebuild my life and overcome the trauma. I am profoundly grateful to Andrew. I understand he was simply doing his job, and seeking legal counsel can be daunting, but Andrew and Kris Greenwood, thank you both sincerely from the bottom of my heart.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. We're so glad that we were able to provide the support and advocacy you needed during such a difficult time. We are grateful to know that our efforts helped you move forward and reunite with your children. We appreciate your trust and are here to help whenever you may need us in the future.

reviews.text: The moment I met Mohammad and brought him on our team, I was relieved. Mohammad was extremely professional and very well spoken. He received my case last minute and spent the entire night preparing to argue in my favor. His efforts ultimately resulted in my case getting dismissed. This is how hiring a lawyer should be. Thank you Mohammad!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience! We're so glad our team was able to achieve a successful outcome for you. Your recommendation means a lot to us, and we're grateful for the opportunity to have assisted you.

reviews.text: Christopher Ross is amazing! There were some setbacks due to the other party, but Chris was incredibly patient and kept pushing for my case to keep moving even though we went literally for months with few to no responses from the other party. He really knows his stuff and let me know of every  option available and respected my wishes. Any questions I had were answered very quickly. Thank you so much for all your time and patience Chris!
reviews.ownerResponse: We're glad to know that you felt well-supported and fully informed throughout your case, even with the delays caused by the other party. Your thoughtful words are appreciated, and we are grateful for the opportunity to have assisted you.

reviews.text: Kris Greenwood has been phenomenal! I can’t say enough good things about him and the work he has done for my case. Kris is incredibly thorough and professional and has been so well prepared for each hearing we have had. I always knew exactly what he was working on and what I needed to be working on and our phone calls and meetings were always  organized and productive. It feels like you are his only client the way he pours his focus and effort into your case. Kris is the best of the best!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your thoughtful review! We're so glad to hear that our preparation, organization, and focus made such a difference for you. We appreciate your trust in us and your kind recommendation!
Thank you for your five-star rating! We appreciate you!

reviews.text: Mohammed was a life saver can't thank him enough!! I was taken back to court unfairly and didn't know where to turn. He jumped right on the case keeping me in the loop the hole way he fought for me like a warrior and kept me out of the Cort room thanks guys 👍
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience. We're so glad our team was able to step in quickly, keep you informed, and fight for the positive outcome you deserved. Your trust means a lot to us, and we're grateful for the opportunity to have assisted you!

reviews.text: He is amazing and I'm so glad I found him. He always thought of me and my daughter. Thank you so much Kris Ross
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your five-star rating!

reviews.text: Just another family law firm supporting a broken legal system.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Paul, we were very appreciative of the five-star review you left a few months ago. Your kind words mean a lot to us:

"We couldn't be happier with the team and especially Mr.Rand Lunceford! Rand has worked countless hours helping our son with his custody case. I could write hours of comments on how he has helped us. I'll keep this short and to the point. If you need a great family law attorney, don't wait to call Rand Lunceford! Thank you again Rand for all your hard work. I know we still have a long road ahead."

Mr. Lunceford remains committed to providing the highest level of legal support and guidance. We understand that the legal system can be challenging, and we strive to help our clients navigate their cases and move forward with confidence. If there are specific concerns or issues that you or your son would like to discuss, please do not hesitate to reach out to us directly. Your feedback is valuable, and we are here to address any questions you may have.

Thank you for your continued trust in our firm.

reviews.text:
Rand was my attorney and he lied to us and ask for us to trust him snd I did. But many deadlines were missed, he missed my court dates I had to go in a lone and was steamed rolled by my ex attorney.  When he came to court nothing I gave him was presented to the court and a po was entered and this has now made it to where I'll most likely never see my kids again. Then he demanded another 25 K  and I asked him for what.   He didn't help with any thing that the court said that i had to do and I didn't understand what was going on and asked for help and remind him that he asked for my trust. And now because I  trusted him I look terrible in the courts and no one can help because my case was grossly mishandled by Mr. Lunceford.
By trusting him I have lost everything.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are sorry to hear that you are unhappy with the legal services provided, and we would like to respond to the points you raised:

Deadlines and Court Dates: There was a hearing for a separate case that you attended without Mr. Lunceford because he was not handling that separate case for you at the time. You were aware that he would not be present for the hearing in the separate case, and you received a continuance at the said hearing. No deadlines were missed in your cases while Mr. Lunceford represented you.

Presentation of Evidence: Mr. Lunceford reviewed the materials you provided and ensured that the relevant information was presented to the court.

Request for Funds: Mr. Lunceford requested additional funds for a trial retainer because trial was the next step in your case. You indicated that you did not want to make any additional payments, so Mr. Lunceford withdrew from your case based on your request.

Communication: Mr. Lunceford maintained regular communication and provided updates and explanations throughout your case. From January through April 2024, Mr. Lunceford received over 100 emails from you, and these were reviewed and responded to. On April 26th, you complained that you had not heard from Mr. Lunceford for a month; however, he had just emailed you twice on April 24th and once on April 25th. Comprehensive letters from Mr. Lunceford explaining the various components of your cases and your options were sent regularly.

We strive to provide the highest quality legal services to our clients and regret that you were dissatisfied despite our efforts. Your mother has apologized for your inappropriate behavior during your last call, but as long as you can be respectful, we are available to discuss any concerns you may have.

reviews.text: My experience with Mr. Mohammad Khan was great. He is very knowledgeable and provided me with valuable, personalized advice. He was very organized and knowledgeable. Additionally, he knows how to lighten the mood, which helped make even the toughest of moments feel less stressful.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your review! We're glad to hear you felt supported with knowledgeable, personalized guidance and that our team helped ease some of the stress during a difficult time. We wish you the very best!

reviews.text: I had the privilege of working with Attorney Mohammad, and I can't recommend him highly enough. He is incredibly attentive and genuinely cares about his clients. It is clear that he is sharp and relentless. His communication and attention to detail really helped put my mind at ease. If you need a dedicated and strategic advocate, he's the one you want on your side.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind words and for taking the time to leave this review. We appreciate your confidence and support!

reviews.text: Rand Lunceford and his team have been working diligently to help me out on a difficult custody situation. I had previously spoken to him by phone last year in August about the possibility of bringing the matter before the courts, and he told me to simply let him know once I was ready. When matters came to a head in the first week of February, I knew I needed counsel and I needed it quick. I called his office that morning and they transferred me to Rand immediately. I explained my situation and he actually recalled our 15 minute conversation from 6 months prior. That spoke volumes to me. Not only did he make the time to take my phone call that day, but he was able to schedule a meeting with me within 3 hours where I got to meet with him and the second attorney on the case, Amanda Garske. I was a mess of a father as my time with my daughter was at stake. Rand and Amanda were both extremely understanding and ready for a fight. Since that meeting, Rand, Amanda, and Liz have all put in a ton of work for my daughter and I to fight for what is right. Never have they discounted any of my thoughts or views with the situation. They have not pressured me with making decisions, but instead made sure that I am well informed to make the decisions for the most important issue I have ever faced. When dirty games have come up from the other side, they have met together prior to calling me. This has allowed Rand to call and present an already solid plan, and then I am able to make an informed decision on the next step. To say I am grateful for Rand and his team is a gross understatement. I couldn’t do this without them.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience in such detail. We're so glad to hear that Rand and our team were able to provide the support you needed during such an important time. It means a lot to know that you felt heard, informed, and empowered to make decisions, as this is exactly what we strive for in every case. We appreciate your trust and are honored to have stood by your side!

reviews.text: Very experienced, thorough, and organized. Highly recommended
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you very much Dr. Black!  It was our pleasure to help you on this complex divorce case.

reviews.text:
I hired Rand Lunceford for 2 issues. He was referred to me. He represented me in court. I spent hours preparing documentation but it was hardly used. The other attorney spoke up against the person in court, and he wasn't even my attorney.

He didn't share findings with me related to the case that the other side had, that would've had a big impact on my decision to hire him.

He also sent me documents but didn't consult with me on important details (the financials weren't done). I was charged the entire retainer and more.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are sorry that you were dissatisfied with your experience. The DCFS findings were provided to all parties at the same time, shortly before the hearing, so nothing was withheld from you. Your retainer was applied to the time spent on both matters, leaving a small remaining balance of $74.00. Although we work to be as efficient as possible, retainers are applied to the time and work performed and do not always cover the full cost of a case if additional time is needed. We remain committed to providing exceptional service to our clients and regret that this was not your perception.

reviews.text: Rand Lunceford handled my divorce case and him and his team definitely made this process easy for me. He helped me through everything and was clear through every process. A very smart man and they replied quickly to my emails fast. Definitely recommend him to any one in need of help

We highly recommend Becky to anyone in need of mediation services. She is competent, professional, and VERY patient. I was very appreciative of how straightforward this process was made for me by Rebecca Buck. My many questions were promptly answered and the steps explained well. Divorce is stressful even in the best and most amicable circumstances and I'm grateful of how they lessened that for me.

I worked with Mike Forsberg on a custody case regarding my small child. Terrible ATTORNEY I had to fire him and got Andrew he charged $2000 fot less than 2 hours work then sent bogus padded fee when I only met him a half hour and 30 to 40 min in mediation dont get scammed by a ATTORNEY that won't look up case law file motions do nothing but figure a way to pad your bill I wish there was a 0 star

Called and scheduled a consultation, joined the call waited the entire half hour time and no one ever joined, I called the office back after and they said oh sorry he's not available today, even though they had just scheduled me this morning for it. completely wasted my time.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback. We are ethically required to prioritize the work of current clients over consultations. We appreciate your patience, and hope you will consider rescheduling your FREE consultation for a future date and time.

reviews.text: I was nervous to start a custody change. The consultation went well and gave me the confidence and direction I needed to start the process. I was told what to expect during each step of the process. I felt like any questions I had were answered timely and efficiently. I hope I don’t have to revisit a custody arrangement but if I do I’ll be back here.
It was nice to work with this law firm. Rebecca was a pleasure. She answered my questions and explained things to me. I would definitely recommend them to others.

I had a similar experience as Jeremy.  I hired Ammon and was handed over to Chris.  My ex moved making custody impossible.  Chris failed to prepare and clearly didn't even read the information I put hours of work into.   I provided info as it happened while my ex moved the first time and then again while we had the case pending.   He forgot several details and did not follow through on many of the things he said he would do.

After our case rather than respond to my concerns about being unprepared Chris went silent on the phone for what seemed an eternity, and finally dismissed my concerns by saying I was just upset.

Chris was unprepared and unorganized.   I was very disappointed.
Wonderful to work with and very proficient with their work.  Easy to talk to and fast turn around

These guys were amazing. Becky, Ammon, and Chris were very quick, efficient and I couldn't have asked for a better team to have in my corner. Highly recommend to anyone that's looking for a divorce attorney. Becky was always there whenever I had any questions via phone and email. And if she didn't know an answer, she was quick to figure it out.
Ammon Nelson Law from start to finish did an amazing job with what I needed. Very thorough and affordable. Chris Evans was attentive and was always communicating with me in a timely manner.

Initially my experience was a good one. Ammon is great, and very knowledgeable. I was excited to work with him. Once we got to proceedings and mediation however, I was turned over to Chris who does the litigation. I was very unimpressed and always felt unprepared for what was coming.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback Kelly. We are always seeking to improve and provide our very best service. We wish you the best!

reviews.text: Thank you all at Ammon Nelson Law, PLLC for the fantastic work that the team did to help me get my divorce finalized in mediation. It was handled with true professionalism. The attorney and paralegal team that helped me are  very friendly and informative, helping me  understand all of my options. Michael Forsberg and Rebecca Buck are the attorney's that handled my divorce and they do tremendous work. I highly recommend them if you need excellent divorce attorney's that can get you the outcome that you want. Thank you both again, your experience, guidance, and wisdom are truly appreciated.
I had a professional experience with my attorney Mike and he help me through a difficult divorce. Thank you

Where to start. My divorce took over two years. They agreed to take payment after my marital home sold because of this and I was not working and they failed to file temporary orders to protect me financially. They put a lien on my exes home NOT mine. I missed court because they failed to tell me. I have been divorced before it has never been like this. Also they tack on an 18 percent interest rate on late fees every month of ur bill. So 10k is $1800 a month. I am in a worse position now than before my divorce. They are not honest. They blamed me for not responding when they told me my mediation and divorce was gonna be final months in. So what was the hold up? I never received my decree in the mail. You'd think after all the money I agreed to pay of the house selling (including what I already paid!) they could do that. They will not fight for you or protect you. Hire someone else! Ammon also was my attorney. Told me Mike was filling in one day and never was back on my case. No wonder they were so disorganized pondimg clients off. Such a joke! Go on fb they advocate joint custody. I should have seen this as the biggest red flag. 🤣💀 I'm in so much debt because of this firm. It's sad when your ex husband attorney sends there condolences. Also I was told that my own attorney was talking crap on me to my exes attorney. Absolutely love they have wrecked my life right now with two children. Hope karma comes around. The only nice thing I have to say is that Rebecca the paralegal she's was always nice and kind to me and trying to follow up with my attorney(s).

Ammon & his team are great. I had a feeling I needed to start a will & trust, and never done this or know anything about it. Called him up, very knowledgeable, and helpful thru out the process. I told him what I wanted, he asked me some questions and got what I needed and more. If you need some help, got questions or just need to get started, this is the place to go
Ammon is an organized, efficient, and competent attorney.

Bushell was supposed to be my public defender. I was eligible because my hours were cut short due to the pandemic. Mr. Bushell kept pushing my case aside for over a year. I told him I was ready to take to trial months before. He mentioned he was trying to get a better deal from the prosecutor. I noticed he does that with a lot of his clients. After having Mr. Bushell for over a year he then tells me I’m no longer eligible for a public defender. It was a waste of over a year. He could of been honest and said hey I’m too busy to represent you or something like that instead of wasting that much time.
reviews.ownerResponse: Guillermo, I was appointed to represent you right as Covid began shutting down the courts and our availability to try cases.  I never pushed your case aside, as I was unable to get a trial date for your case as you requested.  However,  at that time, the courts were very limited on times for trial and initially were only doing trial for in-custody defendants.   I also fail to see how my trying to get you a better deal from the prosecutor is a negative for you.  Part of my contact to provide indigent defense for Davis County requires me to continually check that one qualifies for public defender assistance. Initially you did qualify for our services, but your work hours improved and you no longer qualified.  Never once was I too busy to represent you.  I am bound by statute to represent to the court if someone no longer qualifies.  I was ready and prepared to try your case, but you no longer qualified.   No one wasted your time as there was nothing we could do as you did not want to take a deal and a trial could not be set.

reviews.text: They helped me through my entire (long) process and always called me back promptly if I had questions. The entire office was very supportive and understanding and always made me feel valid.
Nice helpful people.
The whole staff was wonderful.  They helped me get through a stressful situation, with kindness and understanding.  I'm very grateful for their services and for going above and beyond.

RJB is one of the worst lawyers you could waste your money on. Buck up and pay good money for a real lawyer, he didn’t fight for ANYTHING for us, not even bail ! And his staff was extremely rude on multiple occasions. Unwilling to get us a billable statement(s) . Many many more issues …. Waisted our money for sure !
reviews.ownerResponse:
Alicia,
As I am sure you realize, you were not my client. As such, the attorney/client relationship precludes me from providing certain information to you. As you were not the one to pay my retainer fee, and the fact that I did not initially have permission to speak to you concerning the case, I could not provide you any information regarding the payment provided to me for my services. I was in fact never paid in full for all the work I provided.
As I tried to continually explained to my client and you, an immigration hold was placed upon him, and because of that, arguing for bail in the state case was pointless because he was not going to be released. He would have been wasting his money in the State system. Moreover, his State case was being screened for Federal charges and he was ultimately charged in the Federal system.
I disagree with your statements that I did not fight for anything. I met with him multiple times, bringing and paying for an interpreter in the jail, and speaking with witnesses, prosecutors, his family in Mexico, etc. The case ultimately was brought to the Federal system, and I do not handle Federal cases. I take my job very seriously and do everything I can within the confines of the law to provide all my clients with the very best legal representation.
My staff is extremely professional and I believe you are upset because of the attorney/client privilege that precludes them from discussing the case when you called. They were not being rude. They were following my directions and that of the Utah Rules of Professional Conduct. That being said, I am more than happy to discuss the matter further with you in an attempt to try and answer any questions you may have regarding my representation.

reviews.text: The lady who answeres the phone is very rude and unprofessional. Its a shame that she is working for a law firm, she has even hung up on clients. She says she will take a message unsure if the attorney even gets them, or maybe she gives them when she sees fit. Never get to talk to the attorney unless you make an appointment, yes i can understand why but if you have some information that pertains to the case and not wanting to wait weeks to speak with him
reviews.ownerResponse:
Amanda,
I received every message you have left regarding the case. As you were not the client, my staff is very limited in what they can and cannot tell you. The attorney/client relationship precludes myself and my staff from providing certain information without permission from the client. That permission I did not receive. I know that my paralegal did not hang up on you, and that she has never hung up on anyone. We recognize that it is a stressful time in both the client's life and their loved ones when one is facing serious criminal charges. However, we strive to provide the very best service to our clients and those who are standing by them. You have to realize that our office is extremely busy and we are sought after to represent individuals who are charged with a crime. That is why I request my staff to set appointments for me so that I can take the time to speak to someone regarding a case. I do know that I have never made anyone wait more than a day or so to speak with me and if it is indeed an emergency, I will take the call as soon as I can.
If you have further concerns, I am more than happy to address them with you. Please call my office at your convenience at 801-612-9505.

reviews.text: This man seriously handles things appropriately. Actually uses his head and fights. Whether it needs a little calm discussion to point out reasonable facts to get the prosecutor off of the mode of a, "I'm going to get this guy because." People generally just settle if they reduce the charge and/or probation. If it is illegitimate and should not be charged with anything in the first place, he works for you and takes care of it. And will get for you, the charges dropped or dismissed. I did do wrong but they tried to enhance it. The enhancement charge I was not guilty of. He very quickly made that clear to the prosecutor and judge. Ryan will also be stern and aggressive on a professional level. Definitely a great attorney that seems to care and gives everything straight forward. Does not over charge at all. Especially for how well and logical he will put things to the prosecutor.

reviews.text:
I found Ryan almost seven years ago when I was facing one of the hardest decisions of my life and needed a divorce attorney. Like many people making that call, I was overwhelmed and not in the best frame of mind. From our very first phone conversation, Ryan treated me with kindness, respect, and empathy. He listened to me as a person, not just as a case. He made time in his schedule to meet with me for a consultation and patiently explained the legal process in a way that helped me understand what to expect and made me feel less alone.

Ryan is not what most people picture when they think of a lawyer. He is incredibly intelligent, thoughtful, and prepared, but also compassionate and deeply human. Throughout an extremely difficult divorce and custody battle, he fought tirelessly for me and my children. He was always prepared, an exceptional writer, and never missed a deadline. In court he was calm, professional, and respectful, but he also knew exactly when and how to speak up and advocate for my family.

What truly sets Ryan apart is that he never treated my case like “just another file.” Over the past seven years he wasn’t just my lawyer—he became my biggest advocate and a dear friend. He believed in me, stood by me during some of the most difficult moments of my life, and never stopped fighting for my children.

There were times when it felt like everything was falling apart and we truly believed all hope was lost. But Ryan never gave up. He stayed committed, kept fighting, and believed that the right outcome was still possible. In the end, what matters most is that we did find our way to a better outcome for my children.

I will always be grateful that I found Ryan, and I truly don’t believe it was a coincidence. There are not enough words to describe what a remarkable attorney and person he is.

Some attorneys handle cases. Ryan cares about people. That is makes him extraordinary.

Hollie Sandberg

If you're like me and have no legal knowledge, I highly recommend the lawyers who handled my car accident case. They were honest, responsible, attentive to every detail, and above all, focused on their clients' well-being. I would choose them again and again. They helped me so much that my life changed thanks to them. God bless them always.

Aland Stanger & Pettitt are an amazing firm to work with. We have needed Ryan and Tim's services over the years, and they both treated our family with respect and understanding through all circumstances. Most recently we worked with Tim Pettitt on my brother's custody case, and he truly treated us like family. He was quick to answer any and all questions whether it was through text or email and was sure to explain everything thoroughly and with care for my brother who was struggling to figure out exactly what his next steps could be and how to handle everything properly. He was honest and upfront about all costs and made sure my brother agreed with everything prior to making any major decisions in the case. He met with us early before every court hearing and was willing to take phone calls and in person meetings when we felt they were necessary. I can't say enough good things about Tim. If you have any family law needs, he is definitely your man. Thank you, Tim, for everything!!!
I worked with Tim on some advice for my divorce.  Amazing attorney and great firm!

If you need a legal teammate(s) to help navigate the tremendous difficulties of divorce and its exceedingly difficult consequences, and desire a legal team that cares about their customer and deliver results, then the BEST legal team in Utah is Tim (and Nikki). After dealing with two other lawyers in Utah that only cared about their fees, and after carefully reviewing their positive Google reviews, I hired Tim to represent my interests.  Their expert legal advice, professionalism, and empathy is truly outstanding. For me, the trauma of divorce was more devastating than my toughest days personally defending our Nation overseas after 9/11. I had no one in my corner until I hired Tim and it's the best decision I made in my life (other than serving our country).  He's earned not only my respect, but my eternal gratitude for his professionalism, competence, and caring. 5 Google stars all the way!!

Not in a million years I thought I would be sitting in a divorce lawyer office.  But Tim was with me the whole time making  me feel comfortable and like we were going to get what I needed no alimony.  Thanks a lot Tim.  Anyone looking for a good counsel he is it!

I unfortunately had an injury claim and had no idea how to go about it or what to do. I was frantic and I knew the financial burden would immensely impact my life. I am forever grateful for contacting Tim and his team. They guided me and were essentially at my beck and call. Their knowledge and experience really made the process a breeze.  They truly care about their clients and give their undivided attention. Half way into my lawsuit, I with bad luck encountered another claim and not once was I questioned or felt like I was the problem. I coincidentally was involved in two claims in just one year. I truly couldn’t have done it without them by my side. Not only did Tim and his team go above and beyond. We also settled with a breeze and an outcome more than expected. I can’t emphasize enough, you will want him by your side when dealing with a personal injury case.

I went into this divorce process very nervous and unsure, but I was quickly put at ease after meeting with Ryan.  I went in the goal of 50/50 custody, everything else was secondary (though still important).  After mediation I was ready to compromise a little bit on the 50/50 just to get things over with, but Ryan wouldn't let me, because he knew how important it was to me when I first came in.  I'm still so grateful for this, that he kept my goals his priority.  In the end, I did get 50/50 and price of the entire process was very fair.  I would recommend this firm to anyone in need.

Melissa is a top tier attorney and I highly recommend her.   She is organized and professional.
